desc: Small, schooling damselfish often found hovering above coral heads.
Famous for their symbiotic relationship with sea anemones.
Known for their vibrant colors and sharp spines near the tail.
Brightly colored reef fish that form large, beautiful schools.
California state marine fish, highly territorial and bright orange.
Long-lived fish that blend into the rocky crevices of the reef.
Changes sex from female to male during its lifecycle. Important for urchin control.
Slender wrasse that acts as a cleaner fish for larger species.
A popular game fish found primarily in kelp forests.
